message:
fix(security): cap all subprocess pipe reads to READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES to prevent OOM

Unbounded read_to_end() calls on subprocess stdout/stderr could exhaust
memory if a child process produces unlimited output (e.g. an infinite
loop printing to stdout, or a tool that dumps a multi-GB file).

Changes:
- extensions.rs: dispatch_hostcall_exec_ref stdout and stderr threads
  now wrap their pipe handles with std::io::Read::take(READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES)
  before calling read_to_end(), capping memory consumption at the
  configured limit per stream
- tools.rs: GrepTool stderr reader and FindTool stdout/stderr readers
  now use .take(READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES) for the same protection
- tools.rs: ReadTool truncation hint message switches from double-quotes
  to single-quotes with proper POSIX escaping (replace ' with '\'')
  to avoid shell injection when the file path contains double-quote
  characters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
